claims.evidence: Single run only (no variance measurement), but qualitative evidence strong: by Phase 7 the change set was essentially determined by accumulated constraints from Phases 1-6. Raw baseline shows dramatically less structure.
claims.strength: Qualitative — needs multiple runs for statistical support

claims.evidence: Greenfield scenario — no existing codebase to index. The sync-spec-code annotations would serve as index in future iterations. Principle validated by architecture: Phase 3-6 loaded no code files.
claims.strength: Architectural — needs brownfield scenario for full test

claims.claim: Cache-optimized loading order produces cache hits
claims.evidence: Cannot measure cache hits in Claude Code sub-agent model. Artifacts were loaded in stable order (vision first, then personas, then specs), but no token-level cache telemetry available.

claims.claim: Review protocol catches errors single-pass misses
claims.evidence: G4 review found 5 findings including SQLite compatibility gap and LinkedIn API risk. A single-pass 'looks good' review would likely miss these because they require cross-referencing tech decisions against deployment mode constraints.
claims.strength: Qualitative — needs adversarial test with planted errors

claims.evidence: Each phase re-read prior artifacts fresh (vision, personas, specs). AC IDs remained consistent across all 19 skills. However, this is a single run — no drift measurement against a checkpoint-less variant.
claims.strength: Consistency evidence strong, comparative evidence missing

claims.claim: Feature/Enabler/Integration cascade discovers full dependency chain
claims.evidence: Journey Layer 3 analysis discovered 5 ungrounded preconditions (auth service, user profile, empty-state handling, content aggregator, trend synthesis engine). Feature Ship Brief identified 5 enablers and 6 integrations via cascade. This is the doctrine's showcase claim for S1.
S1 is a single feature in a single worktree. Need parallel features in separate worktrees to test this claim.
